item.title: The Fall of King Suddhodana
item.description: Today's story will change how you see everything... In the kingdom of Kapilavastu, King Suddhodana ruled with an iron fist, but his heart was consumed by greed for power and wealth. He lavished his people with grand festivals, but at the cost of their freedom. His wife, Queen Maya, gave birth to a son, Siddhartha, who would one day change the course of history. As Siddhartha's beauty and wisdom grew, so did his father's pride and greed. Suddhodana sought to keep his son ignorant of the world's suffering, but fate had other plans. The prince encountered the four sights - old age, sickness, death, and a wandering ascetic - which opened his eyes to the world's true nature. He renounced his royal life and embarked on a spiritual journey, leaving behind the kingdom that had been his prison. This is Greed - the insatiable craving for more. Today we measure success by our bank accounts, asking ourselves, what price are we paying for our prosperity?

item.title: The Tragic Fall of Icarus
item.description: Today's story will change how you see everything... In the golden age of Athens, a young inventor named Icarus lived a life of comfort and excess, the son of the greatest craftsman in the world. Daedalus built the Labyrinth for King Minos of Crete but was imprisoned along with his son. The inventor created two pairs of wings for their escape, warning Icarus to stay in the middle, to stay humble. But Icarus takes flight and feels the rush of power and freedom. The wind obeys him, the clouds part for him, the gods themselves seem small from up here. He climbs higher, higher, the wax softens, one feather falls, then ten, and his wings dissolve entirely. Icarus plunges into the sea that now bears his name. This is Pride - the delusion of invincibility. We all have wings, but our greatest danger lies in flying too close to the sun.
